message:
fix(membrane): return Calibrating when no bundles have frozen baselines in K-theory controller

Previously, the state-assessment loop skipped non-frozen bundles but
never checked whether *any* frozen bundle existed. When all bundles were
still warming up (baseline_frozen == false), every bundle was skipped,
fractured_count and drifting_count remained zero, and the controller fell
through to a non-Calibrating state despite having no evidence to base
a decision on.

Add a `frozen_count` accumulator that increments for each bundle whose
baseline is frozen. After the loop, if frozen_count is zero, return
`KTheoryState::Calibrating` immediately so downstream consumers know
the controller does not yet have enough data to render a verdict. This
prevents false-negative stability assessments during the warm-up phase.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
